title: Final Project Part 2: Analysis and Visualization
description: Analyze the cleaned data, create visualizations, and draw conclusions.

slides.title: Task 3: Clean Up (if needed)
slides.content:
First, ensure data types are correct. For example, convert character columns with few levels to factors. Also, check if 'No phone service' etc. should be a separate level or combined with 'No'.

We'll keep it simple and convert relevant columns to factors.

slides.title: Convert to Factors
slides.codeSnippet:
library(dplyr)
churn_data <- churn_data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), as.factor))
str(churn_data)

slides.title: Task 4: Exploratory Analysis with dplyr
slides.content:
Use dplyr to answer questions like:
- What is the overall churn rate?
- Does churn vary by contract type?
- Are senior citizens more likely to churn?
- How do monthly charges differ between churners and non-churners?

slides.title: Churn Rate by Contract
slides.codeSnippet:
churn_data %>%
  group_by(contract, churn) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(contract) %>%
  mutate(prop = count / sum(count)) %>%
  filter(churn == "Yes") %>%
  select(contract, churn_rate = prop)

quizData.question: Based on the output, which contract type likely has the highest churn rate?
quizData.explanation: Month-to-month contracts usually have highest churn because they're easiest to leave.

slides.title: Task 5: Visualization with ggplot2
slides.content:
Create plots to visualize relationships:
- Bar chart of churn by contract
- Boxplot of monthly charges by churn
- Histogram of tenure, colored by churn

slides.title: Visualize Churn by Contract
slides.codeSnippet:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(churn_data, aes(x = contract, fill = churn)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(y = "Proportion", title = "Churn Proportion by Contract") +
  theme_minimal()

slides.title: Boxplot: Monthly Charges
slides.codeSnippet:
ggplot(churn_data, aes(x = churn, y = monthly_charges, fill = churn)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Monthly Charges by Churn") +
  theme_minimal()

slides.title: Conclusions
slides.content:
Based on your analysis, you might conclude that customers with month-to-month contracts and higher monthly charges are more likely to churn. This could inform business strategies like offering discounts for longer contracts.

**Congratulations!** You've completed a full data analysis project in R. You've imported, cleaned, analyzed, and visualized data – skills that are valuable in any data-related role.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Document your analysis in an R Markdown report to create a reproducible record. You can export to HTML, PDF, or Word.
